title: Home trend – keep the luxury, add colour
description: PROPERTY NEWS - Quiet luxury has long been...
bodyText:
PROPERTY NEWS - Quiet luxury has long been associated with understated elegance — think muted palettes, clean lines, and an emphasis on quality over quantity. But the aesthetic is evolving.

The latest interpretation adds something new: colourful quiet luxury.


> Deep, expressive hues are making their way into carefully curated interiors, without compromising the calm and sophistication that define this timeless style.


It’s a move away from all-neutral schemes and minimalist restraint, without tipping into overwhelming or ostentatious territory.

What makes colourful quiet luxury different?Rather than focusing purely on beige, white or grey, this design style introduces rich tones in measured, meaningful ways. It embraces the emotional quality of colour — how it can anchor a room, evoke a feeling, or reflect the identity of the people who live there.

It’s not about dramatic feature walls or clashing tones. It’s about restraint, subtlety and cohesion.

A muted green velvet armchair in a room of pale plaster walls. A ceiling painted in a moody, charcoal tone that adds quiet drama to a neutral dining space. A touch of saffron or aubergine, used sparingly, but beautifully.

The shift acknowledges that minimalism no longer has to mean monochrome. Colour and quiet can, in fact, coexist.


> Building your base


The foundation of this aesthetic is still rooted in simplicity. Soft whites, taupes, natural wood and gentle stone shades remain essential. These neutrals allow a space to feel open and calming, providing a perfect canvas on which to layer colour.

From there, depth is added through thoughtful choices — a painted bookshelf, a piece of coloured marble, or a richly upholstered chair. These elements add interest without disrupting the room’s overall harmony.

The aim is to introduce tone and texture gradually, so that the space feels collected over time, not overly styled or seasonal.


> Choosing the right colours for your space


In colourful quiet luxury, colour is personal. Instead of chasing trends, the focus is on what works in your environment. Natural light, room function, existing finishes and even geography all play a role in selecting tones that feel considered and balanced.

Earthy shades like rust, clay, forest green and ochre tend to work well in living areas, while cooler hues like powder blue or soft lavender bring a sense of calm to bedrooms and bathrooms.

Deep navy, smoky rose, or moss green can bring richness to cabinetry or soft furnishings without dominating the room.

The key is to choose colours that reflect the mood you want to create — and to apply them in ways that elevate the room, rather than overwhelm it.

Photo: Unsplash


> Texture, quality and intentionality


Colour is only one part of the story. Materiality is just as important. The textures and finishes you choose should echo the same values: craftsmanship, simplicity and longevity.

Natural stone, unpolished wood, textured ceramics, raw linen and brushed metals all contribute to a sense of quiet luxury. Colour applied through these materials — rather than loud prints or synthetic finishes — feels more grounded and sophisticated.

A velvet in a jewel tone. A plaster wall tinted with soft pigment. A ceramic light fitting in a deep, inky glaze. These details bring quiet richness to a space, making it feel luxurious without being obvious.


> A design language that speaks softly — but meaningfully


Ultimately, colourful quiet luxury is about balance. It allows for creativity and character, but always with intention. It’s a reminder that colour doesn’t need to be bold to be beautiful — and that personal expression and timeless style aren’t mutually exclusive.

By building on a neutral base, choosing tones that suit your space, and favouring materials that age well and feel considered, you can create a home that’s both elegant and emotive. A place that reflects your personality, while offering calm, comfort and enduring charm.

In a world where visual noise is everywhere, this softer, warmer approach to luxury offers something refreshing — a moment of stillness, with a hint of colour.

The post Colourful quiet luxury: how to incorporate the new trend in your home appeared first on SA Garden and Home.

Article: Caxton Network News
